task: Investigate Claude Code session handover system gaps and auto-compact behavior by examining binary strings, project metadata, and hook implementations.
discussion_summary: Session focused on reverse-engineering the Claude Code auto-compact mechanism and handover state persistence. Examined binary strings from Claude v2.1.177, inspected ~/.claude/projects metadata, and reviewed post_tool_use.py hook implementation to understand how context remaining is calculated and when compaction triggers. Goal was to build tabular analysis of handover system gaps with concrete evidence from source inspection.

accomplishments.what: Extracted and searched Claude binary for CLAUDE_CODE, CLAUDE_AUTO, CLAUDE_AFT patterns and auto-compact references
Located and inspected ~/.claude/projects metadata structure and project state files
Reviewed post_tool_use.py hook implementation (lines 1-170) to understand hook payload structure
Updated implementation_state.md with session activity log

in_progress.what: Tabular analysis of handover system gaps — partially documented with grep/strings commands executed but analysis not yet synthesized into table format

next_actions.action: Extract and parse the actual post_tool_use.py hook payload structure (lines 80-170) to identify what context metadata is available in PreCompact and PostCompact hooks
next_actions.why: Understanding hook payload is critical to determining what handover data can be captured at compaction time

next_actions.action: Create tabular analysis document mapping: [Hook Type] → [Available Metadata] → [Handover Capability] → [Gap] with concrete evidence from examined files
next_actions.why: Original goal was tabular analysis with evidence; grep/strings commands were reconnaissance, now need synthesis

next_actions.action: Examine ~/.claude/settings.json and project state JSON structure to identify what fields persist across sessions and which are lost at compaction
next_actions.why: Determines what state can be reliably recovered in next session

next_actions.action: Commit implementation_state.md and notifications.log changes once analysis table is complete
next_actions.why: Clean up uncommitted state and preserve session progress

decisions.decision: Focus on hook payload inspection rather than binary reverse-engineering of compaction algorithm
decisions.reason: Hook payloads are more directly actionable for improving handover; binary strings provide limited actionable insight

failed_approaches.approach: Searching Claude binary (strings) for auto-compact logic and context percentage calculations
failed_approaches.reason: Yielded limited actionable results; binary strings are fragmented and lack context. Hook implementation inspection is more direct.

failed_approaches.approach: Grepping for turns_remaining, context_pct, context_remaining patterns across filesystem
failed_approaches.reason: No matches found; these patterns may not exist in codebase or use different naming conventions

relational_files.why: Hook implementation defines what metadata is available at tool use completion and compaction events
State writer likely uses hook payloads to persist handover data
State reader recovers persisted handover data in next session
Contains Claude Code configuration including auto-compact thresholds

blockers: Tabular analysis not yet synthesized from reconnaissance data — need to consolidate findings into structured table with evidence columns
